info.title: Third Party API
info.description: Deriv API provides programmatic access to trading services, account management, and market data across multiple trading platforms including CFDs, options, and peer-to-peer exchange. This REST API enables developers to integrate Deriv's trading infrastructure into their applications with comprehensive endpoints for account operations, trading activities, and real-time market information.

tags.name: Options Trading
tags.description: Options trading API: account management, OTP issuance for WebSocket auth, and WebSocket endpoints for trading and public market data.

tags.name: Options Trading - Legacy
tags.description: Read-only access to a user's pre-upgrade options trading data (statement, accounts, upgrade status). These endpoints expose data sourced from the legacy platform and will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

tags.name: Reporting
tags.description: Aggregated business reporting endpoints (e.g. per-application markup statistics).

tags.name: System
tags.description: Operational endpoints for service health and infrastructure checks.

get.description: Returns every options trading account the authenticated user owns, including both real and demo accounts. Use this to discover the account IDs needed for placing trades, opening WebSocket connections, or resetting demo balances.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

post.description: Opens a new options trading account for the authenticated user. The account type (real or demo) and currency are taken from the request body. The newly created account is returned in the response.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier used for Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication.

200.description: OK - returned when the account already exists (if the same parameters are provided)

201.description: Created - returned when a new account is created

post.description: Resets the balance of the specified demo account back to its default starting amount. Only demo accounts owned by the authenticated user can be reset; calling this on a real account will be rejected.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Options Trading account ID

post.summary: Issue a one-time password for WebSocket authentication
post.description: Issues a short-lived one-time password (OTP) that authenticates a subsequent WebSocket connection for the specified account. The response includes the OTP and a ready-to-use WebSocket URL with the OTP attached as a query parameter. The OTP is valid for 120 seconds and can be used only once — open the WebSocket connection promptly after calling this endpoint.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Options Trading account ID that OTP is requested for

get.summary: Open a WebSocket connection for a demo account
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP request to a WebSocket connection scoped to a demo options trading account. Authentication requires a valid OTP obtained from the OTP endpoint, passed as the `otp` query parameter. Use this channel to subscribe to real-time market data, send trading instructions, and receive order and position updates for the demo account.

parameters.name: otp
parameters.description: One time password to authorize websocket connection. Token should be generated by endpoint /trading/v1/options/accounts/{accountId}/otp

get.summary: Open a WebSocket connection for a real account
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP request to a WebSocket connection scoped to a real-money options trading account. Authentication requires a valid OTP obtained from the OTP endpoint, passed as the `otp` query parameter. Use this channel to subscribe to real-time market data, send trading instructions, and receive order and position updates. Trades placed over this channel settle against the account's real balance.

parameters.name: otp
parameters.description: One time password to authorize websocket connection. Token should be generated by endpoint /trading/v1/options/accounts/{accountId}/otp

get.summary: Open a public WebSocket connection (no authentication)
get.description: Upgrades the HTTP request to a WebSocket connection for public options trading data. No authentication or OTP is required. Use this channel to stream real-time market data — for example quotes, ticks, and contract metadata — without needing a user account. Account-scoped actions such as placing trades or reading balances are not available on this channel.

get.summary: Get the legacy transaction statement for a loginid
get.description: Returns the historical transaction statement from the legacy options platform for the authenticated user, scoped to the default account of the loginid supplied in the `loginid` query parameter. Results can be filtered by transaction type and by a date range, expressed as a half-open interval `[date_from, date_to)` using epoch seconds. Pagination uses `limit` (default 100, maximum 999) and `offset`. Transactions are returned newest first, with the most recent `transaction_time` at the top and ties broken by `id` in descending order. Legacy endpoint — will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

parameters.name: loginid
parameters.description: Legacy loginid whose default-account statement to return. Must match `^[A-Z]+[0-9]+$`.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound as epoch seconds. A row is included iff its true `transaction_time` is `>= date_from`. Defaults to 0 (all history). Values above 2147483647 (the year-2038 epoch limit) are rejected with HTTP 422.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound as epoch seconds. A row is included iff its true `transaction_time` is `< date_to`. Defaults to current time. Values above 2147483647 (the year-2038 epoch limit) are rejected with HTTP 422.

parameters.name: action_type
parameters.description: Filter by transaction type. Defaults to all types.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions to return. Defaults to 100, maximum 999.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of transactions to skip before returning results. Use with `limit` for pagination.

404.description: User or default account not found

409.description: User migration is pending or has failed

get.summary: List the authenticated user's legacy accounts
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's accounts on the legacy options platform, grouped by loginid. Each account includes its `account_id` and `currency`. Loginids that have no accounts are omitted. When the user has no resolvable legacy accounts, the response is `{"loginids": {}}`. Use the returned loginids with the legacy statement endpoint to fetch historical transactions. Legacy endpoint — will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

404.description: User has no legacy account mapping

409.description: User migration is pending or has failed

get.summary: Get the user's platform upgrade status
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's current platform upgrade status. The response always has HTTP 200 and the shape `{"status": <state>}`, where `<state>` is one of `complete`, `pending`, `failed`, or `not_applicable`. These states are normal answers, not error conditions — for example, `pending` means the upgrade is still in progress, and `not_applicable` means the user never had a legacy account in the first place. Responses are returned with `Cache-Control: no-store`, so polling clients always see fresh state. Legacy endpoint — will be removed once the platform upgrade is complete for all users.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier

Cache-Control.description: Always set to `no-store` so polling clients are not served stale state.

get.description: Returns aggregated markup statistics over a date range, broken down per application registered by the authenticated user. Use this to track how much markup revenue each of your registered applications has generated. The date range is specified via query parameters.

parameters.name: Deriv-App-ID
parameters.description: Application identifier used for Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Interval start date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive, UTC.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Interval end date (YYYY-MM-DD), inclusive, UTC.

get.description: Returns HTTP 200 when the gateway is up and able to serve traffic. Intended for use by load balancers, uptime monitors, and infrastructure probes. The endpoint requires no authentication and returns no body payload of interest — the HTTP status alone is the signal.

scopes.account_manage: Write access for account creation and management
scopes.application_read: Read-only access to your registered applications

AppMarkupBreakdownRow.description: Per-application aggregates included in the statistics window.

app_markup_usd.description: Sum of application markup attributed to this app in USD over the requested interval.

volume_usd.description: Sum of contract volume (buy price in USD terms) for this app over the requested interval.

payout_usd.description: Sum of all potential payouts in USD terms for this app over the requested interval.

contract_count.description: Number of contracts included in the aggregates for this app.

client_count.description: Number of distinct client accounts that traded under this app in the interval.

AppMarkupStatistics.description: Markup and trading aggregates for the caller's applications over the requested date range. Root fields sum across all included apps; `breakdown` lists the same metrics per application.

total_app_markup_usd.description: Sum of application markup attributed to all included apps in the interval.

total_volume_usd.description: Sum of contract volume (buy price in USD terms) across all included apps in the interval.

total_payout_usd.description: Sum of all potential payouts (in USD terms) across all included apps in the interval.

total_contract_count.description: Total number of contracts counted toward the aggregates across all included apps.

total_client_count.description: Number of distinct client accounts that traded under any included app in the interval.

breakdown.description: Per-application rows; each entry corresponds to one `app_id` with metrics for that app only.

LegacyAccountItem.description: A single account from the legacy platform.

LegacyAccountsResponse.description: Legacy accounts grouped by loginid. Loginids with no resolvable accounts are omitted.

loginids.description: Map of loginid (e.g. CR0001) to its accounts.

LegacyMigrationStatusResponse.description: Current state of the user's platform upgrade. All known states (including pending, failed, and not_applicable) are returned with HTTP 200.

LegacyTransactionItem.description: A single historical transaction from the legacy platform for the requested loginid's default account.

account_id.description: Legacy account identifier (the default account of the requested loginid).

loginid.description: Legacy client loginid as requested (e.g. CR12345).

amount.description: Signed transaction amount as a decimal string.

balance_after.description: Account balance after the transaction as a decimal string.

transaction_time.description: Transaction timestamp as epoch seconds, computed as the floor of the underlying high-precision database timestamp. Supplying this value as `date_from` on a subsequent request always re-includes the originating row.

contract_id.description: Financial market bet ID; null for non-trading transactions.

currency.description: Account currency code (the default account's currency).

shortcode.description: Legacy contract shortcode; null for non-trading transactions.

bet_class.description: Bet class (e.g. higher_lower_bet, digit_bet, multiplier); null for non-trading transactions.

bet_type.description: Bet type (e.g. CALL, PUT, DIGITOVER); null for non-trading transactions.

symbol.description: Underlying market symbol (e.g. R_100, frxEURUSD); null for non-trading transactions.

app_id.description: Identifier of the third-party application that originated the transaction; null if not recorded.

LegacyStatementResponse.description: Statement of historical transactions from the legacy platform for the requested loginid's default account, ordered by `transaction_time` descending (ties broken by `id` descending).

count.description: Number of items in `transactions` (equals `transactions.length`). Not a total across pages.

timing.description: Time for the API to serve the request in milliseconds.

status.description: HTTP-equivalent status code for this error entry.

code.description: One of the predefined error codes.

message.description: Human-readable description of the error. Intended for logs and developer-facing surfaces; not guaranteed stable across versions.

field.description: Request element that caused the error (query param name, path param name, header name, or dot-joined body path). Omitted when not attributable to a single field.

errors.description: List of Errors from the request
